description_long: This house features a simple, modern design with a combination of gable and hip roofs covered in orange tiles. The exterior walls are primarily light gray, with a section of the property enclosed by a tan boundary wall, suggesting a private yard or garden area. Large windows are visible on one side, providing ample natural light to the interior. The structure appears to have a single main story, with a spacious covered area that could serve as a patio or carport. The entryway is modest, lacking ornate features, and the overall layout is functional and straightforward. The house is likely constructed from concrete or masonry, with glass windows and a tiled roof.
description: A modern, single-story house with orange-tiled gable and hip roofs, light gray walls, and a private enclosed yard. Large windows and a simple entryway highlight its functional design.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the house with a large window and the overhanging orange-tiled roof, as well as part of the enclosed yard.
description_view_1: From this angle, the gable roof and the enclosed green yard are prominent, along with the main entry area and a visible window.
description_view_2: This side highlights the long boundary wall enclosing the yard and the hip roof section, with minimal window visibility.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the plain gable end of the house, with no visible windows or entry points.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple entryway with a single door, no porch, and minimal decorative elements
